Notice Of Hearing On Petition Of Defendants Elizabeth G. Moy, M.D . And Joanne A. Hom, M.D. To Compel Arbitration And Stay Court Proceedings
Off Calendar Oct-07-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. ALEXANDER MACDOUGALL, ESQ. of Donnelly Nelson Depolo & Murray, Tel No. 925-287-8181 appearing for Defendants Elizabeth Moy and Joanne Hom;
Atty. LISA T. UNGERER, ESQ. of Law Offices of Rankin, Sproat, Mires, Reynolds, Shuey & Mintz, Tel No. 510-465-3922 appearing for Defendant Jane Fang, M.D.

RULING - Matter was not argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANTS ELIZABETH MOY AND JOANNE HOM'S Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Court Proceedings, the petition to compel arbitration and stay action filed by defendants Elizabeth Moy and Joanne Hom and joined in by Jane Fang and defendant Leslie Kardos is granted as to all four defendants. The arbitration agreement complies with CCP 1295. Plaintiffs have not shown any basis why it should not be enforced. Plaintiff Jason Buttles is bound by the agreement notwithstanding that he didn't sign it. (Bolanos v. Khalatian (1991) 231 Cal. App. 3d 1586, 1591). The agreement also covers Kardos, Hom and Fang, all of whom either was on call of Moy or assisted her in the medical services that are the subject of this case.(Michaelis v. Shori (1993) 20 Cal. App., 4th 133). CCP 1281.2(c) is inapplicable because the arbitration agreement was made in compliance with CCp 1295. The proposed order submitted by counsel for defendant Leslie Kardos, M.D. is signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/HEK)
